title: pinguicula laueana
intro:
Pinguicula laueana, also known as a butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae, characterized by its unique ability to capture and digest small insects for nutrients.

This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

The health benefits of Pinguicula laueana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a subject of interest in traditional medicine.

Herbal preparations of Pinguicula laueana typically involve infusing the leaves in water or alcohol to create teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
